commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.12: the count — figures roll up the alphabet's reach (#35)

The count node kept its idea and execution but its content was poor: a
release year (not a quantity you accumulate), a vague '60+', and a '1'.
Replace the three figures with the alphabet's own combinatorics — 26
letters, 676 pairs, 17,576 triples — numbers that ARE accumulations and
that enact the gallery's thesis (one idea, many faces) as arithmetic.

The larger two figures derive from the single authored base (base**exp),
so the explosion is one fact, not three literals that could drift
[LAW:one-source-of-truth]. Figures are grouped in thousands for legibility
mid-roll. Mechanism, amber 'data' identity, and brisk read-beat unchanged.
